component_identification.content: Core components from the rulebook include 1 board, 4 player mats, 4 character tiles, 4 player aids, 4 VP markers, 4 income markers, money tokens, 64 location and industry cards, 8 wild cards, 56 link tiles, and 180 industry tiles split by player color. Shared resources are 30 coal cubes, 18 iron cubes, and 15 beer barrels, plus merchant tiles. Industry types are cotton mills, manufacturers, coal mines, iron works, breweries, and potteries.

core_game_loop.content: On your turn discard one card and take two actions, choosing from build, network, develop, sell, loan, or scout. Card restrictions and connectivity determine legal placement. You first play canal era then rail era, with a scoring and transition between eras. Flipped industry tiles generate victory points in scoring. Play continues until players run out of cards for the era.

detailed_rules.content: Card use controls what and where you can build. Location cards enable actions in named cities and industry cards enable a chosen industry in legal connected locations. Build costs money and often consumes coal, iron, or beer from connected supply or market according to priority rules. Network places canal links in era one and rail links in era two with rail fuel costs. Develop removes lower-level tiles by paying iron so stronger tiles become available. Sell flips industry tiles through merchant connections and beer use, creating points and income gains. Loans provide cash but reduce income level. At canal-era end score flipped industries and canal links, then remove canal links and specific low-level unflipped tiles as instructed before rail setup. Common mistakes are illegal disconnected resource sourcing, misusing beer, and forgetting era-specific link scoring.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Brass scores at end of canal era and again at end of rail era. In each scoring, only flipped industry tiles and links for that era score. Money itself is not victory points. Game ends after rail era is exhausted and final scoring is complete. Highest total victory points wins, with money as tie support by standard rules.

beginner_strategy.content: Increase income early so later turns are stronger. Develop with purpose to reach high-value tiles sooner. Build breweries only when you can actually consume beer in selling lines. Watch connectivity before committing coal-dependent builds. In canal era, prioritize flipping a few meaningful tiles over scattered setup.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
